name: gctrl-agent
title: GCTRL Knowledge Agent (Pi)
description: A drop-in data-engineer agent for an agent team. Ingests data, searches and reads access-controlled knowledge graphs and their source text, merges graphs, and surfaces classification conflicts — every result is automatically filtered to the calling token's clearance level and per-graph grants, so the agent can hand teammates exactly the data they're allowed to see and nothing more.

auth.how_to_obtain: Create a scoped Access Token in GCTRL → Access Control → Access Tokens. Pick a base clearance (PUBLIC/INTERNAL/CONFIDENTIAL/STRICTLY_CONFIDENTIAL) and optionally grant access to specific graphs. The token enforces least privilege server-side.

transport.request_body: JSON object of the tool's args
transport.conversational_endpoint: POST {base_url}/agent/chat (SSE) — natural-language access to the same tools

tools.name: list_graphs
tools.description: List knowledge graphs the token can access.

tools.name: get_entity
tools.description: Read one entity and its outgoing connections.

tools.name: search_chunks
tools.description: Retrieve the source-text passages behind a question (RAG retrieval), filtered to the token's clearance. Use this to ground answers and cite sources.

tools.name: list_conflicts
tools.description: List open classification conflicts awaiting review.

tools.name: create_extraction
tools.description: Ingest text into the knowledge graph at a given classification.

tools.name: fuse_graphs
tools.description: Merge graphs by their source job ids; the merge preserves each element's classification.

security_model: Access is enforced at the API, not by convention. A token with PUBLIC clearance physically cannot retrieve INTERNAL+ data through any tool, unless explicitly granted a specific graph. Every tool call is recorded in the audit trail (which token accessed what).
